contentBlocks.original:
कौसल्या सुप्रजा राम पूर्वासन्ध्या प्रवर्तते ।
उत्तिष्ठ नरशार्दूल कर्त्तव्यं दैवमाह्निकम् ॥१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kausalyā suprajā rāma pūrvāsandhyā pravartate .
uttiṣṭha naraśārdūla karttavyaṃ daivamāhnikam ..1..
contentBlocks.translation: O Rama, son of Kausalya, the sun is about to rise in the eastern skies; please arise to offer the early morning oblations.
contentBlocks.meanings: कौसल्या (kausalyā) – Kausalya; सुप्रजा (suprajā) – good progeny (su:good | prajā:progeny); राम (rāma) – Rama; पूर्वासन्ध्या (pūrvāsandhyā) – early morning (pūrva:early | sandhyā:morning); प्रवर्तते (pravartate) – is arising; उत्तिष्ठ (uttiṣṭha) – arise; नरशार्दूल (naraśārdūla) – tiger among men (nara:man | śārdūla:tiger); कर्त्तव्यम् (karttavyam) – duty; दैवमाह्निकम् (daivamāhnikam) – divine daily ritual (daiva:divine | āhnikam:daily ritual);

contentBlocks.original:
उत्तिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ गोविन्द उत्तिष्ठ गरुडध्वज ।
उत्तिष्ठ कमलाकान्त त्रैलोक्यं मङ्गलं कुरु ॥२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
uttiṣṭhottiṣṭha govinda uttiṣṭha garuḍadhvaja .
uttiṣṭha kamalākānta trailokyaṃ maṅgalaṃ kuru ..2..
contentBlocks.translation: Arise O Govinda the holder of flag with Garuda emblem; Cast aside your sleep to bestow your protection and solace to the Universe.
contentBlocks.meanings: उत्तिष्ठ (uttiṣṭha) – arise; उत्तिष्ठ (uttiṣṭha) – arise; गोविन्द (govinda) – Govinda; उत्तिष्ठ (uttiṣṭha) – arise; गरुडध्वज (garuḍadhvaja) – holder of Garuda flag (garuḍa:Garuda | dhvaja:flag); उत्तिष्ठ (uttiṣṭha) – arise; कमलाकान्त (kamalākānta) – beloved of Lakshmi (kamalā:Lakshmi | kānta:beloved); त्रैलोक्यं (trailokyaṃ) – three worlds (tri:three | lokyaṃ:worlds); मङ्गलं (maṅgalaṃ) – auspiciousness; कुरु (kuru) – do;

contentBlocks.original:
मातस्समस्तजगतां मधुकैटभारेः
वक्षोविहारिणि मनोहरदिव्यमूर्ते ।
श्रीस्वामिनि श्रितजनप्रियदानशीले
श्रीवेङ्कटेशदयिते तव सुप्रभातम् ॥३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mātassamastajagatāṃ madhukaiṭabhāreḥ
vakṣovihāriṇi manoharadivyamūrte .
śrīsvāmini śritajanapriyadānaśīle
śrīveṅkaṭeśadayite tava suprabhātam ..3..
contentBlocks.translation: Sri Lakshmi, mother of the world, you reside in the heart of Mahavisnu; O beautiful one that captures the attention of the minds of humans, O bestower of all benefits to those who fall at His feet, O dear and beloved one to Lord Venkateswara, I offer these morning Pranams to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: मातः (mātaḥ) – O mother; समस्तजगताम् (samastajagatām) – of all the worlds (samasta:all | jagatām:of the worlds); मधुकैटभारेः (madhukaiṭabhāreḥ) – of the slayer of Madhu and Kaitabha (madhu:Madhu | kaiṭabha:Kaitabha | āreḥ:of the slayer); वक्षःविहारिणि (vakṣovihāriṇi) – residing in the chest (vakṣaḥ:chest | vihāriṇi:residing); मनोहरदिव्यमूर्ते (manoharadivyamūrte) – O captivating divine form (manohara:captivating | divya:divine | mūrte:form); श्रीस्वामिनि (śrīsvāmini) – O goddess (śrī:goddess | svāmini:O mistress); श्रितजनप्रियदानशीले (śritajanapriyadānaśīle) – O bestower of boons to devotees (śrita:devotees | jana:people | priya:dear | dāna:boon | śīle:nature); श्रीवेङ्कटेशदयिते (śrīveṅkaṭeśadayite) – O beloved of Venkateswara (śrī:goddess | veṅkaṭeśa:Venkateswara | dayite:beloved); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
तव सुप्रभातमरविन्दलोचने
भवतु प्रसन्नमुखचन्द्रमण्डले ।
विधिशङ्करेन्द्रवनिताभिरर्चिते
वृशशैलनाथदयिते दयानिधे ॥४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tava suprabhātamaravindalocane
bhavatu prasannamukhacandramaṇḍale .
vidhiśaṅkarendravanitābhirarcite
vṛśaśailanāthadayite dayānidhe ..4..
contentBlocks.translation: O Devi, good morning to you whose eyes are like the lotus and the bright face of the full moon; the consorts of Brahma, Sankara and Indra pay their respects to you the ocean of mercy dear to the Lord of the Vrishabadri.
contentBlocks.meanings: तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning; अरविन्दलोचने (aravindalocane) – lotus-eyed (aravinda:lotus | locane:eyed); भवतु (bhavatu) – may it be; प्रसन्नमुखचन्द्रमण्डले (prasannamukhacandramaṇḍale) – bright-faced like the full moon (prasanna:bright | mukha:face | candra:moon | maṇḍale:circle); विधिशङ्करेन्द्रवनिताभिः (vidhiśaṅkarendravanitābhiḥ) – by the consorts of Brahma, Sankara, and Indra (vidhi:Brahma | śaṅkara:Sankara | indra:Indra | vanitābhiḥ:by the consorts); अर्चिते (arcite) – respected; वृशशैलनाथदयिते (vṛśaśailanāthadayite) – dear to the Lord of Vrishabadri (vṛśa:Vrisha | śaila:mountain | nātha:Lord | dayite:dear to); दयानिधे (dayānidhe) – ocean of mercy (dayā:mercy | nidhe:ocean);

contentBlocks.original:
अत्र्यादिसप्तऋषयस्समुपास्य सन्ध्यां
आकाशसिन्धुकमलानि मनोहराणि ।
आदाय पादयुगमर्चयितुं प्रपन्नाः
शेषाद्रिशेखरविभो तव सुप्रभातम् ॥५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atryādisaptaṛṣayassamupāsya sandhyāṃ
ākāśasindhukamalāni manoharāṇi .
ādāya pādayugamarcayituṃ prapannāḥ
śeṣādriśekharavibho tava suprabhātam ..5..
contentBlocks.translation: O resident of Sehadri, the seven holy rishis headed by Atri, having performed their morning sandhya, are at your feet offering beautiful lotuses and the waters of heavenly rivers, please receive them.
contentBlocks.meanings: अत्र्यादि (atryādi) – headed by Atri (atri:Atri | ādi:beginning with); सप्तऋषयः (saptaṛṣayaḥ) – seven sages (sapta:seven | ṛṣayaḥ:sages); समुपास्य (samupāsya) – having performed; सन्ध्याम् (sandhyām) – morning sandhya; आकाशसिन्धुकमलानि (ākāśasindhukamalāni) – lotuses of heavenly rivers (ākāśa:heavenly | sindhu:rivers | kamalāni:lotuses); मनोहराणि (manoharāṇi) – beautiful; आदाय (ādāya) – having taken; पादयुगम् (pādayugam) – pair of feet (pāda:feet | yugam:pair); अर्चयितुं (arcayituṃ) – to worship; प्रपन्नाः (prapannāḥ) – have approached; शेषाद्रिशेखरविभो (śeṣādriśekharavibho) – O resident of Sehadri (śeṣa:Sehadri | adri:mountain | śekhara:peak | vibho:O lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
पञ्चाननाब्जभवषण्मुखवासवाद्याः
त्रैविक्रमादिचरितं विबुधाः स्तुवन्ति ।
भाषापतिः पठति वासरशुद्धिमारात्
शेषाद्रिशेखरविभो तव सुप्रभातम् ॥६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pañcānanābja bhava ṣaṇmukhavāsavādyāḥ
traivikramādicaritaṃ vibudhāḥ stuvanti .
bhāṣāpatiḥ paṭhati vāsaraśuddhimārāt
śeṣādriśekharavibho tava suprabhātam ..6..
contentBlocks.translation: All the Gods from Brahma, Shiva, Shanmukha and others are praising your TriVikrama avathara, Brhaspathi, the Purohita of Devas is reading out the Panchangam (almanac), O Lord may this morning be glorious.
contentBlocks.meanings: पञ्चानन (pañcānana) – five-faced; अब्जभव (abjabhava) – born from lotus (abja:lotus | bhava:born); षण्मुख (ṣaṇmukha) – six-faced; वासव (vāsava) – Indra; आद्याः (ādyāḥ) – and others; त्रैविक्रम (traivikrama) – related to Trivikrama; आदि (ādi) – and others; चरितं (caritaṃ) – deeds; विबुधाः (vibudhāḥ) – gods; स्तुवन्ति (stuvanti) – praise; भाषापतिः (bhāṣāpatiḥ) – Bṛhaspati; पठति (paṭhati) – reads; वासरशुद्धिम् (vāsaraśuddhim) – purity of the day (vāsara:day | śuddhim:purity); आरात् (ārāt) – from near; शेषाद्रिशेखरविभो (śeṣādriśekharavibho) – O Lord of Śeṣādri (śeṣādri:Śeṣādri | śekhara:peak | vibho:O Lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
ईषत्प्रफुल्लसरसीरुहनारिकेल
पूगद्रुमादिसुमनोहरपालिकानाम् ।
आवाति मन्दमनिलस्सह दिव्यगन्धैः
शेषाद्रिशेखरविभो तव सुप्रभातम् ॥७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
īṣatpraphullasarasīruhanārikela
pūgadrumādisumanoharapālikānām .
āvāti mandamanilassaha divyagandhaiḥ
śeṣādriśekharavibho tava suprabhātam ..7..
contentBlocks.translation: O Sehadri vasa, the morning scene is beautiful with the tossing of water lillies, cocoanut trees and transplanted young paddy fields, the morning breeze is blowing carrying pleasant smells, good morning to you
contentBlocks.meanings: ईषत् (īṣat) – slightly; प्रफुल्ल (prafula) – blooming; सरसीरुह (sarasīruha) – water lilies; नारिकेल (nārikela) – coconut trees; पूगद्रुम (pūgadruma) – areca trees (pūga:areca | druma:trees); आदि (ādi) – and others; सुमनोहर (sumano'hara) – beautiful; पालिकानाम् (pālikānām) – of the fields; आवाति (āvāti) – blows; मन्द (manda) – gentle; अनिल (anila) – wind; सह (saha) – with; दिव्यगन्धैः (divyagandhaiḥ) – divine fragrances (divya:divine | gandhaiḥ:fragrances); शेषाद्रिशेखरविभो (śeṣādriśekharavibho) – O Lord of Sheshadri (śeṣādri:Sheshadri | śekhara:peak | vibho:O Lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
उन्मील्य नेत्रयुगमुत्तमपञ्जरस्थाः
पात्रावशिष्टकदलीफलपायसानि ।
भुक्त्वा सलीलमथ केलिशुकाः पठन्ति
शेषाद्रिशेखरविभो तव सुप्रभातम् ॥८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
unmīlya netrayugamuttamapañjarasthāḥ
pātrāvaśiṣṭakadalīphalapāyasāni .
bhuktvā salīlamatha keliśukāḥ paṭhanti
śeṣādriśekharavibho tava suprabhātam ..8..
contentBlocks.translation: O Venkateswara, the temple parrots are singing pleasantly after having tasted the fruits, payasa in your Pooja vessels and quenching their thirst; I pray for a good morning to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: उन्मील्य (unmīlya) – having opened; नेत्रयुगम् (netrayugam) – pair of eyes (netra:eye | yugam:pair); उत्तमपञ्जरस्थाः (uttamapañjarasthāḥ) – in the best cage (uttama:best | pañjara:cage | sthāḥ:situated); पात्रावशिष्ट (pātrāvaśiṣṭa) – leftover in vessels (pātra:vessel | avaśiṣṭa:leftover); कदलीफलपायसानि (kadalīphalapāyasāni) – banana fruits and payasa (kadalī:banana | phala:fruit | pāyasāni:payasa); भुक्त्वा (bhuktvā) – having eaten; सलीलम् (salīlam) – pleasantly; अथ (atha) – then; केलिशुकाः (keliśukāḥ) – playful parrots (keli:playful | śukāḥ:parrots); पठन्ति (paṭhanti) – recite; शेषाद्रिशेखरविभो (śeṣādriśekharavibho) – O Lord of Seshadri (śeṣādri:Seshadri | śekhara:peak | vibho:O Lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
तन्त्रीप्रकर्षमधुरस्वनया विपञ्च्या
गायत्यनन्तचरितं तव नारदोऽपि ।
भाषासमग्रमसकृत्करचाररम्यं
शेषाद्रिशेखरविभो तव सुप्रभातम् ॥९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tantrīprakarṣamadhurasvanayā vipañcyā
gāyatyanantacaritaṃ tava nārado'pi .
bhāṣāsamagrama sakṛtkara cāraramyaṃ
śeṣādriśekharavibho tava suprabhātam ..9..
contentBlocks.translation: With his melodious musical instruments Sage Narada is singing your endless stories set to music and dancing to the enchanting music with lovely hand gestures, may this morning be beautiful.
contentBlocks.meanings: तन्त्रीप्रकर्षमधुरस्वनया (tantrīprakarṣamadhurasvanayā) – with melodious musical instruments (tantrī:musical instrument | prakarṣa:excellence | madhura:sweet | svanayā:with sound); विपञ्च्या (vipañcyā) – by Vina (instrument); गायति (gāyati) – sings; अनन्तचरितं (anantacaritaṃ) – endless stories (ananta:endless | caritaṃ:stories); तव (tava) – your; नारदः (nāradaḥ) – Narada; अपि (api) – also; भाषासमग्रम (bhāṣāsamagram) – complete with language (bhāṣā:language | samagram:complete); असकृत् (asakṛt) – repeatedly; करचाररम्यं (karacāraramyaṃ) – lovely hand gestures (kara:hand | cāra:movement | ramyaṃ:lovely); शेषाद्रिशेखरविभो (śeṣādriśekharavibho) – O Lord of Sheshadri (śeṣa:Shesha | adri:mountain | śekhara:peak | vibho:O Lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
भृङ्गावली च मकरन्दरसानुविद्ध
झङ्कारगीतनिनदैस्सह सेवनाय ।
निर्यात्युपान्तसरसीकमलोदरेभ्यः
शेषाद्रिशेखरविभो तव सुप्रभातम् ॥१०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhṛṅgāvalī ca makarandarasānuviddha
jhaṅkāragītaninadaissaha sevanāya .
niryātyupānta sarasī kamalodarebhyaḥ
śeṣādriśekharavibho tava suprabhātam ..10..
contentBlocks.translation: O Lord of Seshagiri, the swarm of bees from the lotus flowers of the nearby tank have come out after tasting the honey and carrying makharanda pollen, waiting for your Dharsan.
contentBlocks.meanings: भृङ्गावली (bhṛṅgāvalī) – swarm of bees (bhṛṅga:bee | āvalī:row or swarm); च (ca) – and; मकरन्दरसानुविद्ध (makarandarasānuviddha) – infused with nectar (makaranda:nectar | rasa:essence | anuviddha:infused); झङ्कारगीतनिनदैः (jhaṅkāragītaninadaiḥ) – with buzzing song sounds (jhaṅkāra:buzzing | gīta:song | ninadaiḥ:with sounds); सह (saha) – together with; सेवनाय (sevanāya) – for service; निर्याति (niryāti) – comes out; उपान्तसरसी (upāntasarasī) – nearby tank (upānta:nearby | sarasī:tank); कमलोदरेभ्यः (kamalodarebhyaḥ) – from lotus flowers (kamala:lotus | udara:inside | ebhyaḥ:from); शेषाद्रिशेखरविभो (śeṣādriśekharavibho) – O Lord of Seshagiri (śeṣa:Sesha | adri:mountain | śekhara:peak | vibho:O Lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
योषागणेन वरदध्नि विमथ्यमाने
घोषालयेषु दधिमन्थनतीव्रघोषाः ।
रोषात्कलिं विदधते ककुभश्च कुम्भाः
शेषाद्रिशेखरविभो तव सुप्रभातम् ॥११॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yoṣāgaṇena varadadhni vimathyamāne
ghoṣālayeṣu dadhimanthanatīvraghoṣāḥ .
roṣātkaliṃ vidadhate kakubhaśca kumbhāḥ
śeṣādriśekharavibho tava suprabhātam ..11..
contentBlocks.translation: O Lord, the milk maids are churning their curd pots and are singing; the two sounds are competing with each other. May this morning be glorious to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: योषागणेन (yoṣāgaṇena) – by the group of women (yoṣā:women | gaṇena:by the group); वरदध्नि (varadadhni) – in the excellent curd (vara:excellent | dadhni:in the curd); विमथ्यमाने (vimathyamāne) – being churned; घोषालयेषु (ghoṣālayeṣu) – in the cow-sheds (ghoṣa:cow | ālaya:sheds | eṣu:in); दधिमन्थनतीव्रघोषाः (dadhimanthanatīvraghoṣāḥ) – intense churning sounds (dadhi:curd | manthana:churning | tīvra:intense | ghoṣāḥ:sounds); रोषात् (roṣāt) – out of anger; कलिं (kaliṃ) – discord; विदधते (vidadhate) – create; ककुभः (kakubhaḥ) – mountains; च (ca) – and; कुम्भाः (kumbhāḥ) – pots; शेषाद्रिशेखरविभो (śeṣādriśekharavibho) – O Lord of Śeṣādri peak (śeṣa:Śeṣa | adri:mountain | śekhara:peak | vibho:O Lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
पद्मेशमित्रशतपत्रगतालिवर्गाः
हर्तुं श्रियं कुवलयस्य निजाङ्गलक्ष्म्या ।
भेरीनिनादमिव बिभ्रति तीव्रनादं
शेषाद्रिशेखरविभो तव सुप्रभातम् ॥१२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
padmeśamitraśatapatragatālivargāḥ
hartuṃ śriyaṃ kuvalayasya nijāṅgalakṣmyā .
bherīninādamiva bibhrati tīvranādaṃ
śeṣādriśekharavibho tava suprabhātam ..12..
contentBlocks.translation: The friends of the bees are trying to steal away the bluish brightness of the flowers and the sound of the drums. May this morning be glorious to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: पद्मेशमित्रशतपत्रगतालिवर्गाः (padmeśamitraśatapatragatālivargāḥ) – friends of the bees (padmeśa:of the lotus | mitra:friend | śatapatra:hundred-petaled | gata:gone to | ali:bee | vargāḥ:group); हर्तुं (hartuṃ) – to steal; श्रियम् (śriyam) – brightness; कुवलयस्य (kuvalayasya) – of the flower; निजाङ्गलक्ष्म्या (nijāṅgalakṣmyā) – with own body beauty (nija:own | aṅga:body | lakṣmyā:beauty); भेरीनिनादमिव (bherīninādamiva) – like the sound of drums (bherī:drum | ninādam:sound | iva:like); बिभ्रति (bibhrati) – carry; तीव्रनादं (tīvranādaṃ) – intense sound (tīvra:intense | nādaṃ:sound); शेषाद्रिशेखरविभो (śeṣādriśekharavibho) – O lord of Śeṣādri (śeṣa:Śeṣa | adri:mountain | śekhara:peak | vibho:O lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – morning;

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीमन्नभीष्टवरदाखिललोकबन्धो
श्रीश्रीनिवास जगदेकदयैकसिन्धो ।
श्रीदेवतागृहभुजान्तरदिव्यमूर्ते
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम् ॥१३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīmannabhīṣṭa varadākhila lokabandho
śrīśrīnivāsa jagadekadayaikasindho .
śrīdevatāgṛhabhujāntaradivyamūrte
śrīveṅkaṭācalapate tava suprabhātam ..13..
contentBlocks.translation: O Srinivasa, bestower of all desired boons, closest kith and kin of the universe, ocean of compassion, O Lord of charming beautiful form in the abode of the Gods, let this morn bring glory to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: श्रीमन् (śrīman) – O auspicious one; अभीष्टवरद (abhīṣṭavarada) – bestower of desired boons (abhīṣṭa:desired | varada:bestower of boons); अखिललोकबन्धो (akhilalokabandho) – kin of the universe (akhila:entire | loka:world | bandho:kin); श्रीश्रीनिवास (śrīśrīnivāsa) – O Srinivasa (śrī:O | śrīnivāsa:Srinivasa); जगदेकदयैकसिन्धो (jagadekadayaikasindho) – ocean of compassion (jagat:world | eka:one | dayā:compassion | eka:one | sindhu:ocean); श्रीदेवतागृहभुजान्तरदिव्यमूर्ते (śrīdevatāgṛhabhujāntaradivyamūrte) – Lord of charming form (śrī:O | devatā:gods | gṛha:abode | bhuja:arm | antara:within | divya:divine | mūrti:form); श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते (śrīveṅkaṭācalapate) – O Lord of Venkata Hill (śrī:O | veṅkaṭa:Venkata | acala:hill | pate:Lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीस्वामिपुष्करिणिकाप्लवनिर्मलाङ्गाः
श्रेयोऽर्थिनो हरविरिञ्चसनन्दनाद्याः ।
द्वारे वसन्ति वरवेत्रहतोत्तमाङ्गाः
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम् ॥१४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrī svāmi puṣkariṇikāplava nirmalāṅgāḥ
śreyo'rthino hara viriñca sanandanādyāḥ .
dvāre vasanti varavetra hatottamāṅgāḥ
śrīveṅkaṭācalapate tava suprabhātam ..14..
contentBlocks.translation: Having taken their holy dip in the Swami pushkarini, Gods like Brahma and Siva and sages such as Sanandana are waiting at the door for your Dharsan with their hands raised holding the rattan cane (for controlling the crowds). Glorious morning to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: श्री (śrī) – glory; स्वामि (svāmi) – lord; पुष्करिणिका (puṣkariṇikā) – pond; प्लव (plava) – bath; निर्मलाङ्गाः (nirmalāṅgāḥ) – with pure bodies (nirmala:pure | aṅgāḥ:bodies); श्रेयोऽर्थिनः (śreyo'rthinaḥ) – seekers of welfare (śreyaḥ:welfare | arthinaḥ:seekers); हर (hara) – Shiva; विरिञ्च (viriñca) – Brahma; सनन्दनाद्याः (sanandanādyāḥ) – Sanandana and others (sanandana:Sanandana | ādyāḥ:and others); द्वारे (dvāre) – at the door; वसन्ति (vasanti) – reside; वरवेत्र (varavetra) – best cane (vara:best | vetra:cane); हत (hata) – held; उत्तमाङ्गाः (uttamāṅgāḥ) – with raised hands (uttama:raised | aṅgāḥ:hands); श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते (śrīveṅkaṭācalapate) – Lord of Venkata Hill (śrī:glory | veṅkaṭa:Venkata | acala:hill | pate:lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीशेषशैल गरुडाचलवेङ्कटाद्रि
नारायणाद्रि वृषभाद्रिवृषाद्रि मुख्याम् ।
आख्यां त्वदीयवसतेरनिशं वदन्ति
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम् ॥१५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīśeṣaśaila garuḍācalaveṅkaṭādri
nārāyaṇādri vṛṣabhādrivṛṣādri mukhyām .
ākhyāṃ tvadīyavasateraniśaṃ vadanti
śrīveṅkaṭācalapate tava suprabhātam ..15..
contentBlocks.translation: Glory to you O Venkatesa, whose abode is described incessantly in the Holy books, variously as Seshachala, Garudachala, Venkatadri, Narayanadri, Vrishabahadri and Vrishadri.
contentBlocks.meanings: श्रीशेषशैल (śrīśeṣaśaila) – Śeṣa mountain (śrī:glorious | śeṣa:Śeṣa | śaila:mountain); गरुडाचल (garuḍācala) – Garuda hill (garuḍa:Garuda | ācala:hill); वेङ्कटाद्रि (veṅkaṭādri) – Venkata mountain (veṅkaṭa:Venkata | adri:mountain); नारायणाद्रि (nārāyaṇādri) – Narayana mountain (nārāyaṇa:Narayana | adri:mountain); वृषभाद्रि (vṛṣabhādri) – Vrishabha mountain (vṛṣabha:Vrishabha | adri:mountain); वृषाद्रि (vṛṣādri) – Vrisha mountain (vṛṣa:Vrisha | adri:mountain); मुख्याम् (mukhyām) – chief; आख्यां (ākhyām) – name; त्वदीय (tvadīya) – your; वसतेः (vasateḥ) – abode; अनिशं (aniśam) – incessantly; वदन्ति (vadanti) – they say; श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते (śrīveṅkaṭācalapate) – Lord of Venkata hill (śrī:glorious | veṅkaṭa:Venkata | ācala:hill | pate:Lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
सेवापराश्शिवसुरेशकृशानुधर्म
रक्षोऽम्बुनाथ पवमान धनादिनाथाः ।
बद्धाञ्जलि प्रविलसन्निजशीर्ष देशाः
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम् ॥१६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sevāparāśśiva sureśa kṛśānudharma
rakṣo'mbunātha pavamāna dhanādināthāḥ .
baddhāñjali pravilasannijaśīrṣa deśāḥ
śrīveṅkaṭācalapate tava suprabhātam ..16..
contentBlocks.translation: O Lord! desirous of serving you, Siva and Indra with their heads bowed and Kubera the lord of wealth and the protectors of the eight directions (Dikpalakas) with their hands tied in front in reverence are waiting for you.
contentBlocks.meanings: सेवापराः (sevāparāḥ) – desirous of serving (sevā:service | parāḥ:desirous); शिव (śiva) – Shiva; सुरेश (sureśa) – lord of gods (sura:gods | īśa:lord); कृशानु (kṛśānu) – Agni (fire god); धर्म (dharma) – righteousness; रक्षः (rakṣaḥ) – Rakshasas; अम्बुनाथ (ambunātha) – lord of waters (ambu:water | nātha:lord); पवमान (pavamāna) – wind god; धनादिनाथाः (dhanādināthāḥ) – lords of wealth, etc. (dhana:wealth | ādi:etc. | nāthāḥ:lords); बद्धाञ्जलि (baddhāñjali) – with folded hands (baddha:bound | añjali:hands); प्रविलसन् (pravilasan) – shining; निजशीर्ष (nijaśīrṣa) – own head (nija:own | śīrṣa:head); देशाः (deśāḥ) – regions; श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते (śrīveṅkaṭācalapate) – Lord of Venkata Hill (śrī:revered | veṅkaṭa:Venkata | acala:hill | pate:lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
धाटीषु ते विहगराज मृगाधिराज
नागाधिराज गजराज हयाधिराजाः ।
स्वस्वाधिकार महिमाधिकमर्थयन्ते
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम् ॥१७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhāṭīṣu te vihagarāja mṛgādhirāja
nāgādhirāja gajarāja hayādhirājāḥ .
svasvādhikāra mahimādhika marthayante
śrīveṅkaṭācalapate tava suprabhātam ..17..
contentBlocks.translation: Venkatesa, seeking your benevolent look of approval Garuda and other leaders of your retinue are jostling for your first look to enhance their supervisory authority. Good morning to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: धाटीषु (dhāṭīṣu) – in the flocks; ते (te) – your; विहगराज (vihagarāja) – king of birds (vihaga:bird | rāja:king); मृगाधिराज (mṛgādhirāja) – king of animals (mṛga:animal | adhirāja:supreme king); नागाधिराज (nāgādhirāja) – king of serpents (nāga:serpent | adhirāja:supreme king); गजराज (gajarāja) – king of elephants (gaja:elephant | rāja:king); हयाधिराजाः (hayādhirājāḥ) – kings of horses (haya:horse | adhirāja:supreme king); स्वस्वाधिकार (svasvādhikāra) – own authority (sva:own | adhikāra:authority); महिमाधिकम् (mahimādhikam) – greater glory (mahima:glory | adhikam:greater); अर्थयन्ते (arthayante) – seek; श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते (śrīveṅkaṭācalapate) – Lord of Venkata Hill (śrī:revered | veṅkaṭa:Venkata | acala:hill | pate:lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
सूर्येन्दु भौम बुध वाक्पति काव्यसौरि
स्वर्भानु केतु दिविषत्परिषत्प्रधानाः ।
त्वद्दास दास चरमावधि दासदासाः
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम् ॥१८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sūryendu bhauma budha vākpati kāvyasauri
svarbhānu ketu diviṣatpariṣatpradhānāḥ .
tvaddāsa dāsa caramāvadhi dāsadāsāḥ
śrīveṅkaṭācalapate tava suprabhātam ..18..
contentBlocks.translation: O Lord, the over lords of the nine planets,(Sun, Moon, Mangala, Budha, Guru,Shukra, Sani, Rahu,Kethu) are ready waiting to offer you their service.
contentBlocks.meanings: सूर्येन्दु (sūryendu) – Sun and Moon (sūrya:Sun | indu:Moon); भौम (bhauma) – Mars; बुध (budha) – Mercury; वाक्पति (vākpati) – Jupiter; काव्यसौरि (kāvyasauri) – Venus and Saturn (kāvya:Venus | sauri:Saturn); स्वर्भानु (svarbhānu) – Rahu; केतु (ketu) – Ketu; दिविषत्परिषत्प्रधानाः (diviṣatpariṣatpradhānāḥ) – chiefs of celestial assembly (diviṣat:celestial beings | pariṣat:assembly | pradhānāḥ:chiefs); त्वद्दास (tvaddāsa) – your servants (tvat:your | dāsa:servants); दास (dāsa) – servants; चरमावधि (caramāvadhi) – until the end (carama:end | avadhi:until); दासदासाः (dāsadāsāḥ) – servants of servants (dāsa:servants | dāsāḥ:servants); श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते (śrīveṅkaṭācalapate) – Lord of Venkata Hill (śrī:holy | veṅkaṭa:Venkata | acala:hill | pate:Lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
त्वत्पादधूलि भरितस्फुरितोत्तमाङ्गाः
स्वर्गापवर्ग निरपेक्ष निजान्तरङ्गाः ।
कल्पागमाकलनयाकुलतां लभन्ते
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम् ॥१९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvatpādadhūli bharitasphuritottamāṅgāḥ
svargāpavarga nirapekṣa nijāntaraṅgāḥ .
kalpāgamākalanayākulatāṃ labhante
śrīveṅkaṭācalapate tava suprabhātam ..19..
contentBlocks.translation: Your devotees having ben purified by the dust of your feet, are serving you with intense devotion forgetting their real interest in Moksha and doubting whether they could get this opportunity in their next birth or not.
contentBlocks.meanings: त्वत् (tvat) – your; पाद (pāda) – feet; धूलि (dhūli) – dust; भरित (bharita) – filled; स्फुरित (sphurita) – shining; उत्तमाङ्गाः (uttamāṅgāḥ) – heads; स्वर्ग (svarga) – heaven; अपवर्ग (apavarga) – liberation; निरपेक्ष (nirapekṣa) – indifferent; निज (nija) – own; अन्तरङ्गाः (antaraṅgāḥ) – inner beings; कल्प (kalpa) – age; आगम (āgama) – arrival; आकलन (ākalana) – calculation; याकुलतां (yākulatāṃ) – anxiety; लभन्ते (labhante) – obtain; श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते (śrīveṅkaṭācalapate) – O Lord of Venkata Hill (śrī:O | veṅkaṭa:Venkata | acala:hill | pate:Lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
त्वद्गोपुराग्रशिखराणि निरीक्षमाणाः
स्वर्गापवर्गपदवीं परमां श्रयन्तः ।
मर्त्या मनुष्यभुवने मतिमाश्रयन्ते
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम् ॥२०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvadgopurāgraśikharāṇi nirīkṣamāṇāḥ
svargāpavargapadavīṃ paramāṃ śrayantaḥ .
martyā manuṣyabhuvane matimāśrayante
śrīveṅkaṭācalapate tava suprabhātam ..20..
contentBlocks.translation: O Venkateswara, people who came interested in Moksha samrajya look at the Vimana (crown) of your temple and want to serve you in this earthly abode forgetting what they came for.
contentBlocks.meanings: त्वत् (tvad) – your; गोपुर (gopura) – temple tower; अग्र (agra) – top; शिखराणि (śikharāṇi) – peaks; निरीक्षमाणाः (nirīkṣamāṇāḥ) – observing; स्वर्गापवर्गपदवीं (svargāpavargapadavīm) – path to heaven and liberation (svarga:heaven | apavarga:liberation | padavīm:path); परमां (paramām) – supreme; श्रयन्तः (śrayantaḥ) – seeking refuge; मर्त्याः (martyāḥ) – mortals; मनुष्यभुवने (manuṣyabhuvane) – in the human world (manuṣya:human | bhuvane:in the world); मतिम् (matim) – mind; आश्रयन्ते (āśrayante) – take refuge; श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते (śrīveṅkaṭācalapate) – O Lord of Venkata Hill (śrī:O | veṅkaṭa:Venkata | acala:hill | pate:Lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीभूमिनायक दयादिगुणामृताब्धे
देवाधिदेव जगदेकशरण्यमूर्ते ।
श्रीमन्ननन्तगरुडादिभिरर्चिताङ्घ्रे
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम् ॥२१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrībhūmināyaka dayādiguṇāmṛtābdhe
devādhideva jagadekaśaraṇyamūrte .
śrīmannananta garuḍādibhirarcitāṅghre
śrīveṅkaṭācalapate tava suprabhātam ..21..
contentBlocks.translation: Morning wishes to you Srinivasa; You are the lord of the Mother Earth(Bhoomi), you possess innumerable auspicious qualities like compassion, Lord of all Gods, the only refuge of the universe and waited on at your feet by Nityasuris (eternal companions) like Ananta and Garuda. Good morning.
contentBlocks.meanings: श्रीभूमिनायक (śrībhūmināyaka) – Lord of Mother Earth (śrī:glory | bhūmi:earth | nāyaka:lord); दयादिगुणामृताब्धे (dayādiguṇāmṛtābdhe) – ocean of qualities like compassion (dayā:compassion | ādi:and others | guṇa:qualities | amṛta:nectar | abdhi:ocean); देवाधिदेव (devādhideva) – Lord of all Gods (deva:god | adhi:above | deva:god); जगदेकशरण्यमूर्ते (jagadekaśaraṇyamūrte) – only refuge of the universe (jagat:universe | eka:one | śaraṇya:refuge | mūrti:form); श्रीमन्ननन्तगरुडादिभिरर्चिताङ्घ्रे (śrīmannaṇantagaruḍādibhirarcitāṅghre) – worshipped by Ananta, Garuda, etc. (śrīman:respected | ananta:Ananta | garuḍa:Garuda | ādi:and others | bhiḥ:by | arcita:worshipped | aṅghri:feet); श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते (śrīveṅkaṭācalapate) – Lord of Venkata Hill (śrī:glorious | veṅkaṭa:Venkata | acala:hill | pati:lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीपद्मनाभ पुरुषोत्तम वासुदेव
वैकुण्ठ माधव जनार्दन चक्रपाणे ।
श्रीवत्सचिह्न शरणागतपारिजात
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम् ॥२२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīpadmanābha puruṣottama vāsudeva
vaikuṇṭha mādhava janārdana cakrapāṇe .
śrīvatsacihna śaraṇāgatapārijāta
śrīveṅkaṭācalapate tava suprabhātam ..22..
contentBlocks.translation: O Padmanabha, Purushottama, Vasudeva, Vaikunta,Madhava, identified by your insignias of Srivatsa and the disk(chakra) in your hand, You are like the havenly Parijata tree in granting the wishes of those who surrender at your feet. Good morning to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: श्रीपद्मनाभ (śrīpadmanābha) – O Padmanabha (śrī:O | padmanābha:Padmanabha); पुरुषोत्तम (puruṣottama) – O Purushottama (puruṣa:man | uttama:best); वासुदेव (vāsudeva) – O Vasudeva (vāsu:dwelling | deva:god); वैकुण्ठ (vaikuṇṭha) – O Vaikunta; माधव (mādhava) – O Madhava; जनार्दन (janārdana) – O Janardana; चक्रपाणे (cakrapāṇe) – O holder of the discus (cakra:discus | pāṇi:hand); श्रीवत्सचिह्न (śrīvatsacihna) – marked with Srivatsa (śrīvatsa:Srivatsa | cihna:mark); शरणागतपारिजात (śaraṇāgatapārijāta) – havenly Parijata tree (śaraṇāgata:surrendered | pārijāta:Parijata tree); श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते (śrīveṅkaṭācalapate) – O Lord of Venkata Hill (śrī:O | veṅkaṭa:Venkata | acala:hill | pati:lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
कन्दर्पदर्पहर सुन्दर दिव्यमूर्ते
कान्ताकुचाम्बुरुह कुटमल लोलदृष्टे ।
कल्याणनिर्मलगुणाकर दिव्यकीर्ते
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम् ॥२३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kandarpadarpahara sundara divyamūrte
kāntākucāmburuha kuṭamala loladṛṣṭe .
kalyāṇanirmalaguṇākara divyakīrte
śrīveṅkaṭācalapate tava suprabhātam ..23..
contentBlocks.translation: Your divyamangala vigraha is so beautiful as to vanquish the pride of Manmatha (Cupid), You are renowned in the Worlds for your blemishless auspicious qualities and you cast your blossoming eyesight on the fine parts of Mahalakshmi. Good morning to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: कन्दर्पदर्पहर (kandarpadarpahara) – vanquisher of Cupid's pride (kandarpa:Cupid | darpa:pride | hara:vanquisher); सुन्दर (sundara) – beautiful; दिव्यमूर्ते (divyamūrte) – divine form (divya:divine | mūrte:form); कान्ताकुचाम्बुरुह (kāntākucāmburuha) – on beloved's lotus-like breasts (kāntā:beloved | kuca:breasts | amburuha:lotus-like); कुटमल (kuṭamala) – fine parts; लोलदृष्टे (loladṛṣṭe) – with wandering glance (lola:wandering | dṛṣṭe:glance); कल्याणनिर्मलगुणाकर (kalyāṇanirmalaguṇākara) – mine of pure auspicious qualities (kalyāṇa:auspicious | nirmala:pure | guṇa:qualities | ākara:mine); दिव्यकीर्ते (divyakīrte) – of divine fame (divya:divine | kīrte:fame); श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते (śrīveṅkaṭācalapate) – Lord of Venkata Hill (śrī:holy | veṅkaṭa:Venkata | acala:hill | pate:Lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
मीनाकृते कमठ कोल नृसिंह वर्णिन्
स्वामिन् परश्वथतपोधन रामचन्द्र।
शेषांशराम यदुनन्दन कल्किरूप
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम् ॥२४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mīnākṛte kamaṭha kola nṛsiṃha varṇin
svāmin paraśvathatapodhana rāmacandra.
śeṣāṃśarāma yadunandana kalkirūpa
śrīveṅkaṭācalapate tava suprabhātam ..24..
contentBlocks.translation: O Venktesa you have helped the world by your ten avatars like Matsya, Kurma etc. Good morning to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: मीनाकृते (mīnākṛte) – in the form of fish (mīna:fish | kṛte:in the form of); कमठ (kamaṭha) – tortoise; कोल (kola) – boar; नृसिंह (nṛsiṃha) – man-lion; वर्णिन् (varṇin) – describer; स्वामिन् (svāmin) – O Lord; परश्वथतपोधन (paraśvathatapodhana) – Bhrigu's axe-wielding form (paraśu:axe | atha:and | tapodhana:Bhrigu); रामचन्द्र (rāmacandra) – Rama, the moon-like (rāma:Rama | candra:moon); शेषांशराम (śeṣāṃśarāma) – Rama, part of Shesha (śeṣa:Shesha | aṃśa:part | rāma:Rama); यदुनन्दन (yadunandana) – descendant of Yadu (yadu:Yadu | nandana:descendant); कल्किरूप (kalkirūpa) – form of Kalki (kalki:Kalki | rūpa:form); श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते (śrīveṅkaṭācalapate) – Lord of Venkata Hill (śrī:holy | veṅkaṭa:Venkata | acala:hill | pate:Lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
एला लवङ्ग घनसारसुगन्धितीर्थं
दिव्यं वियत्सरिति हेमघटेषु पूर्णम् ।
धृत्वाऽद्य वैदिक शिखामणयः प्रहृष्टाः
तिष्ठन्ति वेङ्कटपते तव सुप्रभातम् ॥२५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
elā lavaṅga ghanasārasugandhitīrthaṃ
divyaṃ viyatsariti hemaghaṭeṣu pūrṇam .
dhṛtvā'dya vaidika śikhāmaṇayaḥ prahṛṣṭāḥ
tiṣṭhanti veṅkaṭapate tava suprabhātam ..25..
contentBlocks.translation: O Venkatachalapati, brahmins with vedic chants are waiting upon you with pots of Akasa Ganga water scented with cardamom, clove and other spices. May this be a glorious morn to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: एला (elā) – cardamom; लवङ्ग (lavaṅga) – clove; घनसारसुगन्धितीर्थं (ghanasārasugandhitīrthaṃ) – scented holy water (ghana:sandalwood | sāra:essence | sugandhi:fragrant | tīrthaṃ:holy water); दिव्यं (divyaṃ) – divine; वियत्सरिति (viyatsariti) – Akasa Ganga (viyat:sky | sariti:river); हेमघटेषु (hemaghaṭeṣu) – in golden pots (hema:gold | ghaṭeṣu:in pots); पूर्णम् (pūrṇam) – filled; धृत्वा (dhṛtvā) – holding; अद्य (adya) – today; वैदिक (vaidika) – vedic; शिखामणयः (śikhāmaṇayaḥ) – brahmins (śikhā:crest | maṇayaḥ:jewels); प्रहृष्टाः (prahṛṣṭāḥ) – joyful; तिष्ठन्ति (tiṣṭhanti) – stand; वेङ्कटपते (veṅkaṭapate) – O Venkatachalapati (veṅkaṭa:Venkata | pate:lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – glorious morn;

contentBlocks.original:
भास्वानुदेति विकचानि सरोरुहाणि
सम्पूरयन्ति निनदैः ककुभो विहङ्गाः ।
श्रीवैष्णवास्सततमर्थितमङ्गलास्ते
धामाश्रयन्ति तव वेङ्कट सुप्रभातम् ॥२६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhāsvānudeti vikacāni saroruhāṇi
sampūrayanti ninadaiḥ kakubho vihaṅgāḥ .
śrī vaiṣṇavāssatata marthita maṅgalāste
dhāmāśrayanti tava veṅkaṭa suprabhātam ..26..
contentBlocks.translation: The sun has risen and flowers have opened their petals; the chirping of birds is echoed from all directions. Sri Vaishnavas are waiting singing your praise. Let this morning be glorious to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: भास्वान् (bhāsvān) – the sun; उदिति (udeti) – rises; विकचानि (vikacāni) – opened; सरोरुहाणि (saroruḥāṇi) – lotuses; सम्पूरयन्ति (sampūrayanti) – fill; निनदैः (ninadaiḥ) – with sounds; ककुभः (kakubhaḥ) – directions; विहङ्गाः (vihaṅgāḥ) – birds; श्रीवैष्णवाः (śrīvaiṣṇavāḥ) – Sri Vaishnavas (śrī:Sri | vaiṣṇavāḥ:Vaishnavas); सततम् (satatam) – always; अर्थित (arthita) – desired; मङ्गलाः (maṅgalāḥ) – auspicious; ते (te) – your; धाम (dhāma) – abode; आश्रयन्ति (āśrayanti) – take refuge; तव (tava) – your; वेङ्कट (veṅkaṭa) – Venkata; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
ब्रह्मादयस्सुरवरास्समहर्षयस्ते
सन्तस्सनन्दनमुखास्तवथ योगिवर्याः ।
धामान्तिके तव हि मङ्गलवस्तुहस्ताः
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम् ॥२७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brahmādayas sura varās sa maharṣayaste
santas sanandana mukhās tavatha yogi varyāḥ .
dhāmāntike tava hi maṅgalavastuhastāḥ
śrīveṅkaṭācalapate tava suprabhātam ..27..
contentBlocks.translation: O Venkatesa, Gods like Brahma, sadhus and yogeeswaras like Sanaka and Sanantana, are waiting at your gates bearing auspicous Pooja materials. Morning glory to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: ब्रह्मादयः (brahmādayaḥ) – Brahma and others (brahma:Brahma | ādayaḥ:and others); सुरवराः (suravarāḥ) – best among gods (sura:god | varāḥ:best); समहर्षयः (samaharṣayaḥ) – with great sages (sama:with | ṛṣayaḥ:sages); ते (te) – they; सन्तः (santaḥ) – saints; सनन्दनमुखाः (sanandanamukhāḥ) – headed by Sanandana (sanandana:Sanandana | mukhāḥ:headed by); तव (tava) – your; अथ (atha) – and; योगिवर्याः (yogivaryāḥ) – best among yogis (yogi:yogi | varyāḥ:best); धामान्तिके (dhāmāntike) – near the abode (dhāma:abode | antike:near); तव (tava) – your; हि (hi) – indeed; मङ्गलवस्तुहस्ताः (maṅgalavastuhastāḥ) – holding auspicious items (maṅgala:auspicious | vastu:items | hastāḥ:holding); श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते (śrīveṅkaṭācalapate) – O Lord of Venkata Hill (śrī:O | veṅkaṭa:Venkata | acala:hill | pate:Lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – morning glory;

contentBlocks.original:
लक्ष्मीनिवास निरवद्यगुणैकसिन्धो
संसार सागर समुत्तरणैकसेतो ।
वेदान्तवेद्यनिजवैभव भक्तभोग्य
श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते तव सुप्रभातम् ॥२८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakṣmīnivāsa niravadyaguṇaikasindho
saṃsāra sāgara samuttaraṇaikaseto .
vedāntavedyanijavaibhava bhaktabhogya
śrīveṅkaṭācalapate tava suprabhātam ..28..
contentBlocks.translation: O Lord the abode of Lakhmi, the ocean of good qualities, the only boat to cross the ocean of Samsara, the true knowledge sought by Vedanta(Upanishads), the lord of ture glory enjoyed by devotees,glory to you
contentBlocks.meanings: लक्ष्मीनिवास (lakṣmīnivāsa) – abode of Lakshmi (lakṣmī:Lakshmi | nivāsa:abode); निरवद्यगुणैकसिन्धो (niravadyaguṇaikasindho) – ocean of good qualities (niravadya:faultless | guṇa:qualities | eka:one | sindhu:ocean); संसार (saṃsāra) – worldly existence; सागर (sāgara) – ocean; समुत्तरणैकसेतो (samuttaraṇaikaseto) – only bridge to cross (samuttaraṇa:crossing | eka:one | setu:bridge); वेदान्तवेद्यनिजवैभव (vedāntavedyanijavaibhava) – true knowledge sought by Vedanta (vedānta:Vedanta | vedya:known | nija:own | vaibhava:glory); भक्तभोग्य (bhaktabhogya) – enjoyed by devotees (bhakta:devotee | bhogya:enjoyed); श्रीवेङ्कटाचलपते (śrīveṅkaṭācalapate) – Lord of Venkata Hill (śrī:Lord | veṅkaṭācala:Venkata Hill | pati:lord); तव (tava) – your; सुप्रभातम् (suprabhātam) – good morning;

contentBlocks.original:
इत्थं वृषाचलपतेरिह सुप्रभातं
ये मानवाः प्रतिदिनं पठितुं प्रवृत्ताः ।
तेषां प्रभातसमये स्मृतिरङ्गभाजां
प्रज्ञां परार्थसुलभां परमां प्रसूते ॥२९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
itthaṃ vṛṣācalapateriha suprabhātaṃ
ye mānavāḥ pratidinaṃ paṭhituṃ pravṛttāḥ .
teṣāṃ prabhātasamaye smṛtiraṅgabhājāṃ
prajñāṃ parārthasulabhāṃ paramāṃ prasūte ..29..
contentBlocks.translation:
(Salutations to Sri Govinda) Thus ends the Suprabhatam of the lord of Vrishachala hill here,
Those persons who will engage themselves in reciting this everyday with devotion,
And at dawn will worship Sri Govinda with mind and body and with intense devotion,
For them the highest attainment of spiritual wisdom will become easy by the grace of the supreme awakened in their hearts.
contentBlocks.meanings: इत्थं (itthaṃ) – thus; वृषाचलपतेः (vṛṣācalapateḥ) – of the lord of Vrishachala (vṛṣa:bull | acala:mountain | pateḥ:of the lord); इह (iha) – here; सुप्रभातं (suprabhātaṃ) – auspicious morning (su:good | prabhātaṃ:morning); ये (ye) – those who; मानवाः (mānavāḥ) – humans; प्रतिदिनं (pratidinaṃ) – every day; पठितुं (paṭhituṃ) – to recite; प्रवृत्ताः (pravṛttāḥ) – engaged; तेषां (teṣāṃ) – of them; प्रभातसमये (prabhātasamaye) – at dawn (prabhāta:morning | samaye:at time); स्मृतिः (smṛtiḥ) – remembrance; अङ्गभाजां (aṅgabhājāṃ) – of those with bodies (aṅga:body | bhājāṃ:of those having); प्रज्ञां (prajñāṃ) – wisdom; परार्थसुलभां (parārthasulabhāṃ) – easily attainable for others (para:others | artha:purpose | sulabhāṃ:easily attainable); परमां (paramāṃ) – supreme; प्रसूते (prasūte) – produces;

contentBlocks.original:
अथ वेङ्कटेशस्तोत्रम्
कमलाकुच चूचुक कुङ्कमतो
नियतारुणि तातुल नीलतनो ।
कमलायत लोचन लोकपते
विजयीभव वेङ्कट शैलपते ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atha veṅkaṭēśastotram
kamalākucha chūchuka kuṅkamatō
niyatāruṇi tātula nīlatanō ।
kamalāyata lōchana lōkapatē
vijayībhava vēṅkaṭa śailapatē ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Reddened by the saffron from Lakshmi's breasts, of dark blue form, with eyes wide as the lotus, lord of all — O lord of the Venkata hill, be victorious!

contentBlocks.original:
सचतुर्मुख षण्मुख पञ्चमुख
प्रमुखा खिलदैवत मौलिमणे ।
शरणागत वत्सल सारनिधे
परिपालय मां वृष शैलपते ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sachaturmukha ṣaṇmukha pañchamukha
pramukhā khiladaivata mauḻimaṇē ।
śaraṇāgata vatsala sāranidhē
paripālaya māṃ vṛṣa śailapatē ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O crest-jewel of all the gods including four-faced Brahma and six-faced Kumara, O ocean of essence, refuge of those who surrender — protect me, O lord of Vrishadri.

contentBlocks.original:
अतिवेलतया तव दुर्विषहै
रनु वेलकृतै रपराधशतैः ।
भरितं त्वरितं वृष शैलपते
परया कृपया परिपाहि हरे ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ativēlatayā tava durviṣahai
ranu vēlakṛtai raparādhaśataiḥ ।
bharitaṃ tvaritaṃ vṛṣa śailapatē
parayā kṛpayā paripāhi harē ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I have committed countless offences repeatedly, O lord of Venkata — forgive me swiftly with your supreme compassion, O Hari.

contentBlocks.original:
अधि वेङ्कट शैल मुदारमते-
र्जनताभि मताधिक दानरतात् ।
परदेवतया गदितानिगमैः
कमलादयितान्न परङ्कलये ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
adhi vēṅkaṭa śaila mudāramatē-
rjanatābhi matādhika dānaratāt ।
paradēvatayā gaditānigamaiḥ
kamalādayitānna paraṅkalayē ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: On the generous Venkata hill, among all the liberal givers, praised by the Vedas as the supreme deity — I know no greater god than the beloved of Kamala (Vishnu).

contentBlocks.original:
कल वेणुर वावश गोपवधू
शत कोटि वृतात्स्मर कोटि समात् ।
प्रति पल्लविकाभि मतात्-सुखदात्
वसुदेव सुतान्न परङ्कलये ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kala vēṇura vāvaśa gōpavadhū
śata kōṭi vṛtātsmara kōṭi samāt ।
prati pallavikābhi matāt-sukhadāt
vasudēva sutānna paraṅkalayē ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Encircled by hundreds of thousands of cowherd maidens captivated by the sweet flute, equal to millions of Kamadevas — I know no greater god than the son of Vasudeva (Krishna).

contentBlocks.original:
अभिराम गुणाकर दाशरथे
जगदेक धनुर्थर धीरमते ।
रघुनायक राम रमेश विभो
वरदो भव देव दया जलधे ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
abhirāma guṇākara dāśarathē
jagadēka dhanurthara dhīramatē ।
raghunāyaka rāma ramēśa vibhō
varadō bhava dēva dayā jaladhē ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Dasharatha's son, treasury of auspicious qualities, sole wielder of the bow in all worlds, O Rama of firm mind, lord of Rama, O all-pervading one — be gracious, O ocean of compassion.

contentBlocks.original:
अवनी तनया कमनीय करं
रजनीकर चारु मुखाम्बुरुहम् ।
रजनीचर राजत मोमि हिरं
महनीय महं रघुराममये ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
avanī tanayā kamanīya karaṃ
rajanīkara chāru mukhāmburuham ।
rajanīchara rājata mōmi hiraṃ
mahanīya mahaṃ raghurāmamayē ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I meditate on Rama who grasps the tender lotus hand of Sita, moon-faced, annihilator of the proud rakshasas — the great and glorious Raghava.

contentBlocks.original:
सुमुखं सुहृदं सुलभं सुखदं
स्वनुजं च सुकायम मोघशरम् ।
अपहाय रघूद्वय मन्यमहं
न कथञ्चन कञ्चन जातुभजे ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sumukhaṃ suhṛdaṃ sulabhaṃ sukhadaṃ
svanujaṃ cha sukāyama mōghaśaram ।
apahāya raghūdvaya manyamahaṃ
na kathañchana kañchana jātubhajē ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Charming-faced, a true friend, easily approachable, bliss-giving, with a noble younger brother, of beautiful form, unfailing arrows — I worship none but the two Raghavas.

contentBlocks.original:
विना वेङ्कटेशं न नाथो न नाथः
सदा वेङ्कटेशं स्मरामि स्मरामि ।
हरे वेङ्कटेश प्रसीद प्रसीद
प्रियं वेङ्कटॆश प्रयच्छ प्रयच्छ ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vinā vēṅkaṭēśaṃ na nāthō na nāthaḥ
sadā vēṅkaṭēśaṃ smarāmi smarāmi ।
harē vēṅkaṭēśa prasīda prasīda
priyaṃ vēṅkaṭeśa prayaccha prayaccha ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: There is no lord for me other than Venkatesha; I always remember Venkatesha. O Hari Venkatesha, be gracious; O Venkatesha, grant me what I cherish.

contentBlocks.original:
अहं दूरदस्ते पदां भोजयुग्म
प्रणामेच्छया गत्य सेवां करोमि ।
सकृत्सेवया नित्य सेवाफलं त्वं
प्रयच्छ प्रयच्छ प्रभो वेङ्कटेश ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ahaṃ dūradastē padāṃ bhōjayugma
praṇāmēchChayā gatya sēvāṃ karōmi ।
sakṛtsēvayā nitya sēvāphalaṃ tvaṃ
prayaccha prayaccha prabhō vēṅkaṭēśa ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Having come from afar, eager to bow at your lotus feet, I render this service. Grant me the fruit of constant eternal service for this single act of devotion, O lord Venkatesha.

contentBlocks.original:
अज्ञानिना मया दोषा न शेषान्विहितान् हरे ।
क्षमस्व त्वं क्षमस्व त्वं शेषशैल शिखामणे ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ajñāninā mayā dōṣā na śēṣānvihitān harē ।
kṣamasva tvaṃ kṣamasva tvaṃ śēṣaśaila śikhāmaṇē ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Hari, in my ignorance I have committed countless offences. Forgive me, forgive me, O crest-jewel of Sheshadri.

contentBlocks.original:
अथ वेङ्कटेशप्रपत्ति
ईशानां जगतोऽस्य वेङ्कटपते र्विष्णोः परां प्रेयसीं
तद्वक्षःस्थल नित्यवासरसिकां तत्-क्षान्ति संवर्धिनीम् ।
पद्मालङ्कृत पाणिपल्लवयुगां पद्मासनस्थां श्रियं
वात्सल्यादि गुणोज्ज्वलां भगवतीं वन्दे जगन्मातरम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atha veṅkaṭēśaprapatti
īśānāṃ jagatō'sya vēṅkaṭapatē rviṣṇōḥ parāṃ prēyasīṃ
tadvakṣaḥsthala nityavāsarasikāṃ tat-kṣānti saṃvardhinīm ।
padmālaṅkṛta pāṇipallavayugāṃ padmāsanasthāṃ śriyaṃ
vātsalyādi guṇōjjvalāṃ bhagavatīṃ vandē jaganmātaram ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Shri (Lakshmi), the supreme beloved of Venkatesha Vishnu, who ever delights in dwelling on his chest, who fosters his grace, whose hands hold the lotus, who is seated on the lotus — the divine mother of all worlds, radiant with qualities of affection.

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीमन् कृपाजलनिधे कृतसर्वलोक
सर्वज्ञ शक्त नतवत्सल सर्वशेषिन् ।
स्वामिन् सुशील सुल भाश्रित पारिजात
श्रीवेङ्कटेशचरणौ शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīman kṛpājalanidhē kṛtasarvalōka
sarvajña śakta natavatsala sarvaśēṣin ।
svāmin suśīla sula bhāśrita pārijāta
śrīvēṅkaṭēśacharaṇau śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Srinivasa, ocean of compassion, creator of all worlds, all-knowing, mighty, compassionate to those who bow, supreme master of all, of gentle and accessible nature, refuge of the surrendered — I take refuge at your feet.

contentBlocks.original:
आनूपुरार्चित सुजात सुगन्धि पुष्प
सौरभ्य सौरभकरौ समसन्निवेशौ ।
सौम्यौ सदानुभनेऽपि नवानुभाव्यौ
श्रीवेङ्कटेश चरणौ शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ānūpurārchita sujāta sugandhi puṣpa
saurabhya saurabhakarau samasannivēśau ।
saumyau sadānubhanē'pi navānubhāvyau
śrīvēṅkaṭēśa charaṇau śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Adorned with anklets and honoured by flowers of beautiful fragrance, matched in exquisite proportion, ever gentle — yet revealing ever-new wonder to those who meditate eternally — I take refuge at the feet of Venkatesha.

contentBlocks.original:
सद्योविकासि समुदित्त्वर सान्द्रराग
सौरभ्यनिर्भर सरोरुह साम्यवार्ताम् ।
सम्यक्षु साहसपदेषु विलेखयन्तौ
श्रीवेङ्कटेश चरणौ शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sadyōvikāsi samudittvara sāndrarāga
saurabhyanirbhara sarōruha sāmyavārtām ।
samyakṣu sāhasapadēṣu vilēkhayantau
śrīvēṅkaṭēśa charaṇau śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Instantly blooming, intensely fragrant, deep red in hue, surpassing the beauty of the lotus, leaving their mark of auspiciousness wherever they step — I take refuge at the feet of Venkatesha.

contentBlocks.original:
रेखामय ध्वज सुधाकलशातपत्र
वज्राङ्कुशाम्बुरुह कल्पक शङ्खचक्रैः ।
भव्यैरलङ्कृततलौ परतत्त्व चिह्नैः
श्रीवेङ्कटेश चरणौ शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rēkhāmaya dhvaja sudhākalaśātapatra
vajrāṅkuśāmburuha kalpaka śaṅkhachakraiḥ ।
bhavyairalaṅkṛtatalau paratattva chihnaiḥ
śrīvēṅkaṭēśa charaṇau śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Adorned with auspicious marks — the flag, umbrella, thunderbolt, goad, lotus, wish-granting tree, conch and discus — marks of the supreme reality itself — I take refuge at the feet of Venkatesha.

contentBlocks.original:
ताम्रोदरद्युति पराजित पद्मरागौ
बाह्यैर्-महोभि रभिभूत महेन्द्रनीलौ ।
उद्य न्नखांशुभि रुदस्त शशाङ्क भासौ
श्रीवेङ्कटेश चरणौ शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tāmrōdaradyuti parājita padmarāgau
bāhyair-mahōbhi rabhibhūta mahēndranīlau ।
udya nnakhāṃśubhi rudasta śaśāṅka bhāsau
śrīvēṅkaṭēśa charaṇau śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Glowing red within, they outshine the ruby outside; their nails radiate light that eclipses the moon — I take refuge at these feet of Venkatesha.

contentBlocks.original:
स प्रेमभीति कमलाकर पल्लवाभ्यां
संवाहनेऽपि सपदि क्लम माधधानौ ।
कान्ता नवाङ्मानस गोचर सौकुमार्यौ
श्रीवेङ्कटेश चरणौ शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sa prēmabhīti kamalākara pallavābhyāṃ
saṃvāhanē'pi sapadi klama mādhadhānau ।
kāntā navāṅmānasa gōchara saukumāryau
śrīvēṅkaṭēśa charaṇau śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: So tender that Lakshmi's lotus hands cause them pain when massaging with love — yet they are the sublime object of the noble-minded's deepest feeling — I take refuge at the feet of Venkatesha.

contentBlocks.original:
लक्ष्मी मही तदनुरूप निजानुभाव
नीलादि दिव्य महिषी करपल्लवानाम् ।
आरुण्य सङ्क्रमणतः किल सान्द्ररागौ
श्रीवेङ्कटेश चरणौ शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakṣmī mahī tadanurūpa nijānubhāva
nīlādi divya mahiṣī karapallavānām ।
āruṇya saṅkramaṇataḥ kila sāndrarāgau
śrīvēṅkaṭēśa charaṇau śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Made richly red by the touch of the soft hands of Lakshmi, Bhu Devi, Nila Devi and the other divine consorts — I take refuge at the feet of Venkatesha.

contentBlocks.original:
नित्यानमद्विधि शिवादि किरीटकोटि
प्रत्युप्त दीप्त नवरत्नमहः प्ररोहैः ।
नीराजनाविधि मुदार मुपादधानौ
श्रीवेङ्कटेश चरणौ शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nityānamadvidhi śivādi kirīṭakōṭi
pratyupta dīpta navaratnamahaḥ prarōhaiḥ ।
nīrājanāvidhi mudāra mupādadhānau
śrīvēṅkaṭēśa charaṇau śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whose radiance, from the gems in the crowns of ever-bowing Brahma, Shiva and others, performs a ceremonial wave-offering (nirajanaa) — I take refuge at the feet of Venkatesha.

contentBlocks.original:
"विष्णोः पदे परम" इत्युदित प्रशंसौ
यौ "मध्व उत्स" इति भोग्य तयाऽप्युपात्तौ ।
भूयस्तथेति तव पाणितल प्रदिष्टौ
श्रीवेङ्कटेश चरणौ शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
"viṣṇōḥ padē parama" ityudita praśaṃsau
yau "madhva utsa" iti bhōgya tayā'pyupāttau ।
bhūyastathēti tava pāṇitala pradiṣṭau
śrīvēṅkaṭēśa charaṇau śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Praised as "the supreme abode of Vishnu" and celebrated as "the spring of honey" — pointed out again to you by your own hand — I take refuge at the feet of Venkatesha.

contentBlocks.original:
पार्थाय तत्-सदृश सारधिना त्वयैव
यौ दर्शितौ स्वचरणौ शरणं व्रजेति ।
भूयोऽपि मह्य मिह तौ करदर्शितौ ते
श्रीवेङ्कटेश चरणौ शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pārthāya tat-sadṛśa sāradhinā tvayaiva
yau darśitau svacharaṇau śaraṇaṃ vrajēti ।
bhūyō'pi mahya miha tau karadarśitau tē
śrīvēṅkaṭēśa charaṇau śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: These feet which you yourself — acting as Arjuna's charioteer — showed to Arjuna saying "take refuge at my feet"; those same feet you now show to me with your own hand — I take refuge at the feet of Venkatesha.

contentBlocks.original:
मन्मूर्थ्नि कालियफने विकटाटवीषु
श्रीवेङ्कटाद्रि शिखरे शिरसि श्रुतीनाम् ।
चित्तेऽप्यनन्य मनसां सममाहितौ ते
श्रीवेङ्कटेश चरणौ शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
manmūrthni kāḻiyaphanē vikaṭāṭavīṣu
śrīvēṅkaṭādri śikharē śirasi śrutīnām ।
chittē'pyananya manasāṃ samamāhitau tē
śrīvēṅkaṭēśa charaṇau śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Placed on my head, on the hood of Kaliya, in the dense forest, on the summit of Venkatadri, on the crowns of the Vedas, and equally in the heart of those of single-pointed mind — I take refuge at the feet of Venkatesha.

contentBlocks.original:
अम्लान हृष्य दवनीतल कीर्णपुष्पौ
श्रीवेङ्कटाद्रि शिखराभरणाय-मानौ ।
आनन्दिताखिल मनो नयनौ तवै तौ
श्रीवेङ्कटेश चरणौ शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
amlāna hṛṣya davanītala kīrṇapuṣpau
śrīvēṅkaṭādri śikharābharaṇāya-mānau ।
ānanditākhila manō nayanau tavai tau
śrīvēṅkaṭēśa charaṇau śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Feet on which unfading fresh flowers are ever scattered, which are the supreme ornament of Venkata's summit, which fill all minds and eyes with bliss — I take refuge at the feet of Venkatesha.

contentBlocks.original:
प्रायः प्रपन्न जनता प्रथमावगाह्यौ
मातुः स्तनाविव शिशो रमृतायमाणौ ।
प्राप्तौ परस्पर तुला मतुलान्तरौ ते
श्रीवेङ्कटेश चरणौ शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prāyaḥ prapanna janatā prathamāvagāhyau
mātuḥ stanāviva śiśō ramṛtāyamāṇau ।
prāptau paraspara tulā matulāntarau tē
śrīvēṅkaṭēśa charaṇau śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Drunk first with joy by the surrendered, like a baby at its mother's breast — matchless, yet each foot matching the other in equal splendour — I take refuge at the feet of Venkatesha.

contentBlocks.original:
सत्त्वोत्तरैः सतत सेव्यपदाम्बुजेन
संसार तारक दयार्द्र दृगञ्चलेन ।
सौम्योपयन्तृ मुनिना मम दर्शितौ ते
श्रीवेङ्कटेश चरणौ शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sattvōttaraiḥ satata sēvyapadāmbujēna
saṃsāra tāraka dayārdra dṛgañchalēna ।
saumyōpayantṛ muninā mama darśitau tē
śrīvēṅkaṭēśa charaṇau śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: These feet were shown to me by Saumyopayantri Muni (Tiruvaymozhi Pillai) — one whose pure mind is ever devoted to those lotus feet, whose compassionate gaze rescues souls from samsara — I take refuge at the feet of Venkatesha.

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीश श्रिया घटिकया त्वदुपाय भावे
प्राप्येत्वयि स्वयमुपेय तया स्फुरन्त्या ।
नित्याश्रिताय निरवद्य गुणाय तुभ्यं
स्यां किङ्करो वृषगिरीश न जातु मह्यम् ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīśa śriyā ghaṭikayā tvadupāya bhāvē
prāpyētvayi svayamupēya tayā sphurantyā ।
nityāśritāya niravadya guṇāya tubhyaṃ
syāṃ kiṅkarō vṛṣagirīśa na jātu mahyam ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Lord, with Shri (Lakshmi) as the means and you as the goal — you who are the eternal refuge of those who depend on you, of flawless attributes — may I become your servant, O lord of Vrishagiri, never belonging to myself alone.

contentBlocks.original:
अथ वेङ्कटेशमङ्गलाशासनम्
श्रियः कान्ताय कल्याणनिधये निधयेऽर्थिनाम् ।
श्रीवेङ्कट निवासाय श्रीनिवासाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atha veṅkaṭēśamaṅgaḻāśāsanam
śriyaḥ kāntāya kalyāṇanidhayē nidhayē'rthinām ।
śrīvēṅkaṭa nivāsāya śrīnivāsāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness (mangalam) to the beloved of Shri (Lakshmi), the ocean of auspicious qualities, the refuge of those in need — to Srinivasa, the dweller of Venkata hill, be all auspiciousness.

contentBlocks.original:
लक्ष्मी सविभ्रमालोक सुभ्रू विभ्रम चक्षुषे ।
चक्षुषे सर्वलोकानां वेङ्कटेशाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakṣmī savibhramālōka subhrū vibhrama chakṣuṣē ।
chakṣuṣē sarvalōkānāṃ vēṅkaṭēśāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to Venkatesha, whose eyes are the very vision of all the worlds, captivating with the graceful glances of Lakshmi.

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीवेङ्कटाद्रि शृङ्गाग्र मङ्गलाभरणाङ्घ्रये ।
मङ्गलानां निवासाय श्रीनिवासाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīvēṅkaṭādri śṛṅgāgra maṅgaḻābharaṇāṅghrayē ।
maṅgaḻānāṃ nivāsāya śrīnivāsāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to Srinivasa — whose lotus feet are the ornaments adorning the very summit of the Venkata hill, the abode of all auspiciousness.

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वावयव सौन्दर्य सम्पदा सर्वचेतसाम् ।
सदा सम्मोहनायास्तु वेङ्कटेशाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvāvayava saundarya sampadā sarvachētasām ।
sadā sammōhanāyāstu vēṅkaṭēśāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to Venkatesha, whose beauty of every limb eternally enchants the minds of all beings.

contentBlocks.original:
नित्याय निरवद्याय सत्यानन्द चिदात्मने ।
सर्वान्तरात्मने श्रीमद्-वेङ्कटेशाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nityāya niravadyāya satyānanda chidātmanē ।
sarvāntarātmanē śrīmad-vēṅkaṭēśāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to Venkatesha — the eternal, the flawless, the embodiment of true bliss and pure consciousness, the inner self of all.

contentBlocks.original:
स्वत स्सर्वविदे सर्व शक्तये सर्वशेषिणे ।
सुलभाय सुशीलाय वेङ्कटेशाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
svata ssarvavidē sarva śaktayē sarvaśēṣiṇē ।
sulabhāya suśīlāya vēṅkaṭēśāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to Venkatesha — who is all-knowing of himself, omnipotent, the supreme master of all, yet easily accessible and of gentle disposition.

contentBlocks.original:
परस्मै ब्रह्मणे पूर्णकामाय परमात्मने ।
प्रयुञ्जे परतत्त्वाय वेङ्कटेशाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
parasmai brahmaṇē pūrṇakāmāya paramātmanē ।
prayuñjē paratattvāya vēṅkaṭēśāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to Venkatesha, the supreme Brahman, whose desires are ever fulfilled, the Paramatma, to whom I offer this prayer of auspiciousness.

contentBlocks.original:
आकालतत्त्व मश्रान्त मात्मना मनुपश्यताम् ।
अतृप्त्यमृत रूपाय वेङ्कटेशाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ākālatattva maśrānta mātmanā manupaśyatām ।
atṛptyamṛta rūpāya vēṅkaṭēśāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to Venkatesha — whose form brings insatiable delight even to those who behold him eternally, of the nature of immortal nectar.

contentBlocks.original:
प्रायः स्वचरणौ पुंसां शरण्यत्वेन पाणिना ।
कृपयाऽऽदिशते श्रीमद्-वेङ्कटेशाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prāyaḥ svacharaṇau puṃsāṃ śaraṇyatvēna pāṇinā ।
kṛpayā''diśatē śrīmad-vēṅkaṭēśāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to Venkatesha, who graciously extends his own feet to those who seek refuge, indicating them as the means of salvation.

contentBlocks.original:
दयाऽमृत तरङ्गिण्या स्तरङ्गैरिव शीतलैः ।
अपाङ्गै स्सिञ्चते विश्वं वेङ्कटेशाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dayā'mṛta taraṅgiṇyā staraṅgairiva śītalaiḥ ।
apāṅgai ssiñchatē viśvaṃ vēṅkaṭēśāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to Venkatesha — whose compassionate sidelong glances sprinkle the universe with cool waves of mercy like a river of ambrosia.

contentBlocks.original:
स्रग्-भूषाम्बर हेतीनां सुषमाऽऽवहमूर्तये ।
सर्वार्ति शमनायास्तु वेङ्कटेशाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
srag-bhūṣāmbara hētīnāṃ suṣamā''vahamūrtayē ।
sarvārti śamanāyāstu vēṅkaṭēśāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to Venkatesha, whose divine form bears garlands, ornaments, garments and weapons of dazzling splendour, the remover of all afflictions.

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीवैकुण्ठ विरक्ताय स्वामि पुष्करिणीतटे ।
रमया रममाणाय वेङ्कटेशाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīvaikuṇṭha viraktāya svāmi puṣkariṇītaṭē ।
ramayā ramamāṇāya vēṅkaṭēśāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to Venkatesha — who turned away from Vaikunta to sport with Ramaa on the banks of Svami Pushkarini on Venkatadri.

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीमत्-सुन्दरजा मातृमुनि मानसवासिने ।
सर्वलोक निवासाय श्रीनिवासाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīmat-sundarajā mātṛmuni mānasavāsinē ।
sarvalōka nivāsāya śrīnivāsāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to Srinivasa — who dwells in the heart of the great Acharya Sundara Jamamatri Muni (Tirumazhisai Alvar) and in the hearts of all worlds.

contentBlocks.original:
मङ्गला शासनपरैर्-मदाचार्य पुरोगमैः ।
सर्वैश्च पूर्वैराचार्यैः सत्कृतायास्तु मङ्गलम् ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
maṅgaḻā śāsanaparair-madāchārya purōgamaiḥ ।
sarvaiścha pūrvairāchāryaiḥ satkṛtāyāstu maṅgaḻam ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May all auspiciousness be to the Lord, as proclaimed by the Acharyas — beginning with my own teacher — all the previous Acharyas who offered this sacred mangalasasanam.

contentBlocks.original: श्री पद्मावती समेत श्री श्रीनिवास परब्रह्मणे नमः
contentBlocks.transliteration: śrī padmāvatī samēta śrī śrīnivāsa parabrahmaṇē namaḥ
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Sri Srinivasa, the supreme Brahman, together with Sri Padmavati.
